dsl_rule.translation_notes: Bump-and-run reversal bottom pattern translated as: (1) price above short-term MA indicating upward momentum, (2) volume ratio below 1.0 indicating tapering volume during consolidation, (3) crossover above medium-term MA indicating breakout. Exit uses overbought RSI as reversal signal. Cup-with-handle and rounding bottom formations cannot be directly encoded in indicator logic but are implicit in the consolidation and breakout structure. Average rise of 350% noted in original source.

source_rule_text: Bump-and-run reversal bottom: Upward momentum stalls, prices move sideways gathering strength, then breakout higher with volume confirmation
